basic_details.job_title: IIT Delhi Project Research Scientist I Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.short_information: IIT Delhi is recruiting 01 Project Research Scientist-I for an exciting interdisciplinary project in the Department of Mechanical Engineering focusing on advanced robotic surgical instruments for neurosurgery. This is a prestigious opportunity involving cutting-edge medical technology development.

important_dates.declaration of shortlisted candidates: To be notified on IRD website
important_dates.interview_date: To be informed to shortlisted candidates

age_limit.age_details: Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR), New Delhi

qualification.details: criteria: Details | age limit: As per IIT Delhi / Government of India norms (5% relaxation for SC/ST candidates)
qualification.education_qualification: First Class Post Graduate Degree (including integrated PG degrees) OR Second Class Post Graduate Degree with PhD OR For Engineering/IT/CS - First Class Graduate Degree of Four Years.
qualification.experience: Relevant experience in Mechanical Engineering, Robotics, Mechatronics, or related fields is highly desirable.

selection_process: Candidates will be shortlisted based on qualifications and experience. Only shortlisted candidates will be called for an online interview. Final selection is based on performance in the interview. Names of shortlisted candidates will be displayed on the IRD website: http://ird.iitd.ac.in/shortlisted
how_to_apply: Download Form No. IRD/REC-4 from http://ird.iitd.ac.in/rec
Fill the form with complete educational and experience details.
Send the duly filled form by email to [email protected] with Advertisement No. in the subject line.
Or send by post to: Prof. Sunil Jha, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Delhi, Hauz Khas, New Delhi – 110016.
important_instructions: Appointment is purely contractual and renewable yearly or till project duration. No TA/DA will be paid for attending the interview. IIT Delhi reserves the right to fix higher shortlisting criteria. 5% marks relaxation for SC/ST candidates. Retired government employees may apply as per IRD norms.

content_sections.heading: IIT Delhi Project Research Scientist Recruitment 2026 Notification
content_sections.text: Download IIT Delhi Project Research Scientist Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: IIT Delhi Project Research Scientist Recruitment 2026 Overview
content_sections.text: IIT Delhi is recruiting 01 Project Research Scientist-I for an exciting interdisciplinary project in the Department of Mechanical Engineering focusing on advanced robotic surgical instruments for neurosurgery. This is a prestigious opportunity involving cutting-edge medical technology development.

rows.indian_institute_of_technology_delhi: Development of multi-functional surgical instrumentation including robotic actuators, end-effectors and articulated arms for minimally invasive neurosurgery
Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR), New Delhi

content_sections.heading: IIT Delhi Project Research Scientist Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

rows.event: Last Date for Submission of Application

rows.date: To be notified on IRD website
To be informed to shortlisted candidates

content_sections.heading: Job Description
content_sections.text: The selected candidate will contribute to the development of multi-functional surgical robotic systems, including actuators, end-effectors, and articulated arms for minimally invasive neurosurgery. The role involves design, prototyping, testing, and integration of advanced instrumentation.

content_sections.heading: IIT Delhi Project Research Scientist Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

rows.details: First Class Post Graduate Degree (including integrated PG degrees) OR Second Class Post Graduate Degree with PhD OR For Engineering/IT/CS - First Class Graduate Degree of Four Years.
As per IIT Delhi / Government of India norms (5% relaxation for SC/ST candidates)
Relevant experience in Mechanical Engineering, Robotics, Mechatronics, or related fields is highly desirable.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Candidates will be shortlisted based on qualifications and experience. Only shortlisted candidates will be called for an online interview. Final selection is based on performance in the interview. Names of shortlisted candidates will be displayed on the IRD website: http://ird.iitd.ac.in/shortlisted

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for IIT Delhi Project Research Scientist Recruitment 2026
Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Appointment is purely contractual and renewable yearly or till project duration. No TA/DA will be paid for attending the interview. IIT Delhi reserves the right to fix higher shortlisting criteria. 5% marks relaxation for SC/ST candidates. Retired government employees may apply as per IRD norms.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for IIT Delhi Project Research Scientist 2026?
faq.answer: The last date is 29 July 2026 (5:00 PM).

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in IIT Delhi RP04452G Recruitment?
faq.answer: There is 01 vacancy for Project Research Scientist-I.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary for Project Research Scientist-I at IIT Delhi?
faq.answer: Rs. 56,000/- per month plus 30% HRA.

faq.question: Q4. What qualifications are required for IIT Delhi Project Research Scientist post?
faq.answer: First Class PG Degree or equivalent as per the detailed advertisement.

faq.question: Q5. Is PhD mandatory for this IIT Delhi research scientist position?
faq.answer: No, PhD is not mandatory but gives preference for candidates with Second Class PG.

faq.question: Q6. What is the project title for IIT Delhi Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Development of multi-functional surgical instrumentation including robotic actuators for minimally invasive neurosurgery.

faq.question: Q7. How to apply for IIT Delhi Project Research Scientist vacancy?
faq.answer: Download IRD/REC-4 form and send it by email to [email protected]

faq.question: Q8. Will there be a written test for IIT Delhi Project Appointment?
faq.answer: No written test. Selection is through shortlisting and online interview.

faq.question: Q9. Who is the Principal Investigator for this project?
faq.answer: Prof. Sunil Jha, Department of Mechanical Engineering.

faq.question: Q10. Is this a permanent job at IIT Delhi?
faq.answer: No, it is a contractual project appointment renewable yearly or till project end.
